verses.1: A Song, a Psalm of Asaph. God, do not remain quiet; Do not be silent and, God, do not be still.
verses.2: For behold, Your enemies make an uproar, And those who hate You have exalted themselves.
verses.3: They make shrewd plans against Your people, And conspire together against Your treasured ones.
verses.4: They have said, “Come, and let’s wipe them out as a nation, So that the name of Israel will no longer be remembered.”
verses.5: For they have conspired together with one mind; They make a covenant against You:
verses.6: The tents of Edom and the Ishmaelites, Moab and the Hagrites;
verses.7: Gebal, Ammon, and Amalek, Philistia with the inhabitants of Tyre;
verses.8: Assyria also has joined them; They have become a help to the children of Lot. Selah
verses.9: Deal with them as with Midian, As with Sisera and Jabin at the river of Kishon,
verses.10: Who were destroyed at En-dor, Who became like dung for the ground.
verses.11: Make their nobles like Oreb and Zeeb, And all their leaders like Zebah and Zalmunna,
verses.12: Who said, “Let’s possess for ourselves The pastures of God.”
verses.13: My God, make them like the whirling dust, Like chaff before the wind.
verses.14: Like fire that burns the forest, And like a flame that sets the mountains on fire,
verses.15: So pursue them with Your heavy gale, And terrify them with Your storm.
verses.16: Fill their faces with dishonor, So that they will seek Your name, LORD.
verses.17: May they be ashamed and dismayed forever, And may they be humiliated and perish,
verses.18: So that they will know that You alone, whose name is the LORD, Are the Most High over all the earth.
